source: Metabase table 'Calls' (Analytics DB, table id 12288) - Five9 call log export

metricDefinitions.answered30s: Answered AND Total Queue Time <= 30s (speed of answer)
metricDefinitions.slaHit5min: Any of: answered w/ Total Queue Time<=300s, abandoned w/ Time To Abandon (or Total Queue Time)<=300s, or CBSI transfer w/ Total Queue Time<=300s
metricDefinitions.slaPct: slaHit5min / total (all calls in period), per the % of ALL calls resolved within 5 min definition

dataQualityNote: A large share of Total Queue Time / Time To Abandon values in the source table are stored in a corrupted format (comma-decimal scientific notation, e.g. '7,15E+09') rather than valid day-fraction floats. Rows with unparseable timing fields are excluded from the 30s/2min/5min SLA buckets (but still counted in totals/answered/abandoned/CBSI counts, which rely on Talk Time and Disposition, not the corrupted fields). Roughly 60-70% of answered calls in this dataset lack a usable queue-time value, so the speed-of-answer buckets are directionally useful but understate true volume.
coverageNote: The source table currently has no records after 2025-11-03 (last sync). This dataset spans all available history (2024-10-17 to 2025-11-03) as a placeholder/backfill. update_data.py + the daily GitHub Action will begin appending real 2026 rows as soon as the table is re-synced with current data.
